title: trollius palustris
intro: Trollius palustris, also known as the marsh marigold, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating liver and gallbladder issues, due to its ability to stimulate bile production and improve liver function. Trollius palustris has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and supplements.
